description_long: This object is a small, fixed-wing airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a pair of straight wings extending from either side. The wings are mounted at a low position relative to the fuselage, and the tail section features a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The airplane appears to have a single propeller engine mounted at the nose, and the cockpit is located at the front of the fuselage, likely enclosed. The landing gear is visible and appears to be a tricycle configuration with two main wheels and a nose wheel. The surface of the airplane is smooth and likely constructed from lightweight metal or composite materials, with a predominantly white coloration.
description: A small, white, low-wing airplane with a single nose-mounted propeller, tricycle landing gear, and a conventional tail.
description_view_0: From this view, the airplane's nose, propeller, and forward cockpit are most visible, with the wings extending outward and the tail section partially obscured.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the side profile, showing the low-mounted wings, the main landing gear, and the full length of the fuselage.
description_view_2: The rear and side of the airplane are visible, emphasizing the tail assembly and the trailing edges of the wings.
description_view_3: This view provides a clear look at the tail section, including the vertical and horizontal stabilizers, with the wings and nose less prominent.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight configuration with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main wheels and a single nose wheel, fixed.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single canopy.
